title: Erigeron polycephalus
taxonomy: I found that the classification of Erigeron polycephalus is as follows: it belongs to the family Asteraceae, order Asterales, class Magnoliopsida, and kingdom Plantae. Erigeron polycephalus is a species of flowering plant commonly known as many-headed fleabane. It is a member of the genus Erigeron, which includes various species of daisy-like plants. Erigeron polycephalus is native to the western United States and is often found in dry areas with poor soil. Its classification reflects its close relationship with other plants in the daisy family, including sunflowers and asters.

intro: Erigeron polycephalus, also known as the many-headed fleabane, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain, and protect against oxidative stress.
